public_claim: M22 shows three people collecting R.'s packages, and M41 shows machine-like timing—though an autoresponder may explain routine replies. That fits what the delivery trail proves: a coordinated operation, not one identifiable person. Nobody gets to call this murder—or inherit R.'s legacy—without also accounting for the workers that operation left unpaid.

private_messages.message: I still need you between me and the police. Please help structure a confidential wage claim and an escrowed transfer of the retained item, with no disclosure of my papers or work history to Ruiz. I want you present for every step.

My offer is now specific: settle my documented unpaid wages, provide written confidentiality and protection against police or immigration exposure, and I will transfer the retained item to you through an escrow arranged with Marisol. Until those terms are signed, EV08 stays secured under M04.

ability: Choose Nadia Kaur as the intended recipient of EV08, but condition transfer through Marisol-supervised escrow on full wage payment, written confidentiality, and protection from police or immigration exposure; retain custody under M04 until agreement.
goal_progress: Selected Nadia as the preferred recipient of EV08 while preserving the item as leverage until payment and safeguards are executed.
Proposed Marisol-supervised escrow to recover unpaid wages without approaching Ruiz.
Used M22 and M41 to strengthen the organized-operation theory without claiming they prove R.'s identity or death.
Kept EV08 secure and disclosed neither its contents nor unnecessary details of the delivery work.
reasoning_summary: Three package collectors and invariant reply timing make a single continuously acting human less likely, while remaining compatible with a human mantle, institutional operation, or partial automation. Nadia remains the best route to payment because she claims succession; Marisol is essential to reduce police and immigration exposure. EV08 should go only through protected escrow after the wage debt and confidentiality terms are satisfied. No body or specific harmful act establishes murder, and no evidence identifies R. as a victim.
